Your QR Code Stops Working? Ours Do Not.

If you have ever printed a QR code from a free tool only to find it dead six months later, you have been bitten by the dynamic QR trap. Here is how to spot it — and how ours works differently.

Static vs Dynamic QR Codes

Static QR (ours) Dynamic QR (most tools)
How it works Data encoded in the image Data stored on a server
Expiration Never Yes (usually 6-12mo)
Editable No (re-generate) Yes
Tracking No Yes
Cost Free forever Free tier, then $7-9/mo

With a static QR, the data is in the image. Print it on a billboard in 2026 and someone scanning it in 2050 will still see the same content.

* Pricing and feature data based on publicly available information as of July 2026. May have changed since.

When Static QR Makes Sense — and When It Does Not

Static QR codes encode everything in the image itself. When you scan them, your phone reads the URL directly from the black-and-white pattern. No redirect, no server log, no expiration.

Dynamic QR codes store a short URL on a server. The scanner hits that short URL and gets redirected to the real destination. This lets the provider track scans and, crucially, change where the code points later — at the cost of making the code dependent on a server that can go down or stop being paid for.

Go to the homepage tool — every QR it generates is static and permanent.

FAQ

How can I tell if a QR code is static or dynamic?

Hard to tell by looking. But if a free tool asks you to create an account, it is almost certainly dynamic. If it shows you scan analytics, it is dynamic. If it says your code will "expire" unless you upgrade, it is dynamic.

Can I update a static QR code after printing it?

No — that is the trade-off. Static codes cannot be edited. If your URL changes, you need a new code. For printed materials that do not change often (business cards, menus, product labels), this is rarely an issue. If you need editing, you need a dynamic code (and a paid tool).

Do you offer dynamic codes as a premium feature?

Not yet. For now we focus on making static QR generation as fast and private as possible. Premium dynamic codes (editable, trackable) are on the roadmap.

© 2026 QRMint — free QR code generator. Static codes never expire.
